candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label; matches total sales/revenue and is a candidate.

candidates.why: Non-operating gain, not revenue.
Financial income, not operating revenue.

candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.why: Primary direct cost line immediately below revenue; best match for cost of sales.

candidates.why: Broad operating overhead bucket including selling/administrative external costs; suitable SG&A bucket when no explicit SG&A line exists.
Selling cost component, but appears to be a subcomponent of Other External Expenses; using both would likely double count.
Core operating overhead/personnel cost and appropriate SG&A component.
Small residual operating overhead line above operating profit; suitable SG&A component.
Balance sheet asset, not an operating expense.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A row is present. Chose the best overhead buckets while excluding COGS and D&A. Excluded 'Other Selling Expenses' because it appears to sit within 'Other External Expenses' and would likely double count.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Amortization line, not a dedicated current-period R&D expense.
Capitalized balance sheet asset, not R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Capitalized Expenditure for Development, Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Balance sheet contra-asset, not R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Capitalized Expenditure for Development, Net - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balance sheet balancing row, not R&D expense.

variables.notes: No dedicated R&D expense line is shown on the income statement. The only R&D-related line is amortization of previously capitalized R&D, which should not be used as XRD.

candidates.why: Too broad; includes non-interest items such as derivatives/FX and should be avoided when granular interest rows exist.

candidates.why: Recurring borrowing interest expense; strong match for XINT.

candidates.why: Recurring financing cost; appropriate component of XINT.

candidates.why: Income statement profit attribution line, not balance sheet equity.

Share count, not equity.
